query: Are there any resources available for translating Tunisian Arabic dialect that contain both manually translated comments by native speakers and additional data augmented through methods like segmentation at stop words level?
criteria: Tunisian Arabic translation dataset native speaker comments
data augmentation segmentation stop words Arabic dialect translation
Tunisian Arabic dialect parallel corpus augmented translation

title.value: Standardization of Dialect Comments in Social Networks in View of Sentiment Analysis : Case of Tunisian Dialect

m050_resolution_attempt.candidate_title: Enhancing Arabic Dialect Detection on Social Media: A Hybrid Model with an Attention Mechanism
Enhancing Arabic Dialect Detection on Social Media: A Hybrid Model with an Attention Mechanism

recommendation_reason.value: With the growing access to the internet, the spoken Arabic dialect language becomes an informal languages written in social media.

supported_research_question.value: With the growing access to the internet, the spoken Arabic dialect language becomes an informal languages written in social media.

method.value: This linguistic situation inhibits mutual understanding between internet users and makes difficult to use computational approaches since most Arabic resources are intended for the formal language: Modern Standard Arabic (MSA).

data_or_scenario.value: Each of these steps was evaluated on the same test corpus.

main_conclusion.value: Then, the resulting comments are translated using a neural translation model.

limitations.value: In effect, MSA presents the formal language for all Arabic dialects, but they are different at all linguistic levels.

relevance_strength.value: With the growing access to the internet, the spoken Arabic dialect language becomes an informal languages written in social media.

title.value: Sentiment Analysis of Tunisian Dialect: Linguistic Resources and Experiments

m050_resolution_attempt.candidate_title: Sentiment Analysis of Tunisian Dialects: Linguistic Ressources and Experiments
Sentiment Analysis of Tunisian Dialects: Linguistic Ressources and Experiments

recommendation_reason.value: Dialectal Arabic (DA) is significantly different from the Arabic language taught in schools and used in written communication and formal speech (broadcast news, religion, politics, etc.

supported_research_question.value: Dialectal Arabic (DA) is significantly different from the Arabic language taught in schools and used in written communication and formal speech (broadcast news, religion, politics, etc.

method.value: We use Machine Learning techniques to determine the polarity of comments written in Tunisian dialect.

data_or_scenario.value: First, we evaluate the SA systems performances with models trained using freely available MSA and Multi-dialectal data sets.

main_conclusion.value: This corpus shows a significant improvement compared to the best model trained on other Arabic dialects or MSA data.

limitations.value: There are many existing researches in the field of Arabic language Sentiment Analysis (SA); however, they are generally restricted to Modern Standard Arabic (MSA) or some dialects of economic or political interest.

relevance_strength.value: Dialectal Arabic (DA) is significantly different from the Arabic language taught in schools and used in written communication and formal speech (broadcast news, religion, politics, etc.

title.value: Parallel resources for Tunisian Arabic dialect translation

authors_year.value: Saméh Kchaou, Rahma Boujelbane, Lamia Hadrich Belguith (2020)

recommendation_reason.value: The difficulty of processing dialects is clearly observed in the high cost of building representative corpus, in particular for machine translation.

supported_research_question.value: The difficulty of processing dialects is clearly observed in the high cost of building representative corpus, in particular for machine translation.

method.value: Indeed, all machine translation systems require a huge amount and good management of training data, which represents a challenge in a lowresource setting such as the Tunisian Arabic dialect.

data_or_scenario.value: The difficulty of processing dialects is clearly observed in the high cost of building representative corpus, in particular for machine translation.

main_conclusion.value: Obviously, the translation performance is improved by augmenting the size of TD-MSA parallel data sets.

limitations.value: Indeed, all machine translation systems require a huge amount and good management of training data, which represents a challenge in a lowresource setting such as the Tunisian Arabic dialect.

relevance_strength.value: The difficulty of processing dialects is clearly observed in the high cost of building representative corpus, in particular for machine translation.
